commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: It was not by the law of Moses that God followed the practice of the Gentiles, but natural reason itself decrees that a person should live from his labor. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: The apostle says this [here and in Timothy :–] so that Timothy might understand that what he took from those for whom he was, as it were, fighting, and whom he was cultivating as a vine, or feeding as a flock, was not a sign of begging but an acknowledgment of a right.

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: I gain the man by a little self-restraint. "Have we not power to eat and to drink? "

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Even so hath the Lord ordained. S. Luke 10:7; S. Matthew 10:10-11, and Matthew 10:14.
